question_text: Why do LED light bulbs waste less energy than incandescent bulbs?

options.label: B
options.text: They convert a higher proportion of electrical energy into light rather than heat

options.label: C
options.text: They do not produce any heat at all

options.label: D
options.text: They are larger than incandescent bulbs

correct_answer: They convert a higher proportion of electrical energy into light rather than heat
explanation: LED bulbs are more efficient than incandescent bulbs because they convert a larger fraction of the electrical energy they receive into useful light energy, while incandescent bulbs waste a lot of energy as unwanted heat. Both types produce some heat, but LEDs waste much less energy this way.
